title: Am I on TRT? August 2022 bloodwork update

chunks.title: Introduction to Blood Work
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the significance of comprehensive blood work in understanding overall health. They share positive results indicating normal kidney function, uric acid levels, and testosterone levels, suggesting a healthy lifestyle.
chunks.transcript: doing a comprehensive set of blood work really gives you an idea of what's going on in the hood obviously in this situation it's sort of me opening the kimono as you will I believe Tim Ferriss has said that and showing you uh the proof in the pudding that there's no inflammation my kidneys work fine that my uric acid is normal that my testosterone is high that I'm not taking supplements to raise my testosterone that I'm that I'm not doing any funny business behind the scenes to look like a healthy 45 year old which is what I look and feel like at this point in my life so it's fun to pop the hood on my metaphorical car and look in there and see what's going on if you guys want to see other blood work put in the YouTube comments send me a message on Instagram or DM or whatever and I'm happy to get more blood work and keep looking at the

chunks.title: Personal Diet and Supplements
chunks.summary: The speaker describes their animal-based diet and introduces their company, Heart and Soil, which offers supplements made from animal organs. They highlight positive feedback from users benefiting from an animal-based diet.
chunks.transcript: heartansoil.com you can get desiccated organs the amazing thing about histamine and immune is that it has thymus and kidney which are both great sources of Dao diamine oxidase which can help people with allergy issues we also have liver in here other organs that will help support your immune system and histaminergic or allergic type reactions so check out histamine and immune at hardened soil check us out in general all kinds of great organs there to help you reclaim your birth rights Optimal Health it's so cool to see people doing this to benefiting from animal-based diets in general and at heart and soil we have this team a free team of health coaches to support you you can always email us there you don't even have to buy our stuff it's just awesome to be able to do that good work through this cool company heartandsoil.com for checking us out we are here to help you reclaim your Birthright to Optimal Health which we believe you all have a right too on this week's podcast wanted

chunks.title: Comparing Blood Work Results
chunks.summary: The speaker compares blood work results from July and August, discussing variations in testosterone, cholesterol levels, and the impact of dietary changes. They emphasize the importance of insulin sensitivity in interpreting these results.
chunks.transcript: to talk about my August blood work so in July I got some blood work through Wellness FX in August about a month plus later I got some blood work through Merrick Health which is actually one of the sponsors of this podcast how cool is that we did it through LabCorp I got some blood work that I didn't get in July it was also interesting to compare month month I go deeper down the rabbit hole regard to testosterone Section 1 binding globulin Boron genistein isoflavonoids all kinds of good stuff in this podcast I talk a little about tmao and I share my uric acid my ggt all that kind of stuff am I going to get gout from eating a lot of fruit and meat well watch the podcast and find out you can at least get a sense of that based on my uric acid levels in my labs this week so enjoy this none of the blood work podcasts are ever meant to be medical advice but I do hope that me discussing my blood work and showing you what's going on under the hood of this human's physiology is beneficial I also think it's a good way for me to show you the proof in the pudding that has a 45 year old male who takes no performance enhancing drugs no hormones never have the only supplements I take are hard and soil supplements when I can't get fresh organs that I'm doing well and not on trt I'm not on any peptides or anything you can see most of that in my blood work so enjoy this blood work podcast so I got more blood work in August and I wanted to do a follow-up blood work podcast in which I talk about how those two sets of blood work are different individual variations on a day-to-day basis the second set of blood work was a little bit later in the morning so it

chunks.title: Understanding Lipid Levels
chunks.summary: A detailed breakdown of lipid panel results is provided, alongside the speaker's perspective on the implications of elevated LDL cholesterol and overall heart health. Evidence supporting a nuanced view on cholesterol levels is discussed.

chunks.title: Ammonia and Dietary Concerns
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses their ammonia levels in relation to a high-meat diet, addressing common concerns about dietary protein and urea cycles. Their results show low ammonia, countering fears about excessive protein intake.

chunks.title: Iron Studies and Anemia
chunks.summary: The speaker reviews iron levels and other related metrics from their blood work, noting no signs of iron deficiency or anemia. They emphasize the importance of dietary sources of iron and the fluctuations that can occur.
chunks.transcript: looking at my eye iron levels you see that serum iron is 114 the iron saturation is 42 ferritin is 332 vitamin B12 is 1083 folic acid is 15.7 so what do we see there we see that iron is all within normal interestingly my iron saturation is 40 to Now versus I think slightly over 50 percent on the last set of blood work from July one of the reasons I wanted to repeat the blood work was to look at my iron Labs because the July blood work suggested that my iron saturation was slightly high I do not have any genetic predisposition to a condition called hemochromatosis which would suggest an iron accumulation and so I found it interesting that my iron blood work looked like it was a little high from the July blood work you can see here serum iron was 152 that's lower now and higher in July the ferritin was 177 lower in July higher now and my iron saturation was 54. at that time I had hypothesized that perhaps this was related to dehydration and there are a number of change significantly from July to August this is also to say that many of these metrics can change significantly without any significant changes in diet I'm eating essentially exactly the same now as interesting to recheck it again and see if it's down in the 200s or the hundreds perhaps in the future but it's pretty consistently at that level for me which means that I have a lot of iron stored I'm not in danger of iron deficiency anemia nor

chunks.title: Testosterone and Hormonal Health
chunks.summary: A comprehensive discussion of testosterone levels, sex hormone binding globulin, and lifestyle factors impacting hormonal health is provided. The speaker shares insights on the role of exercise and diet in maintaining healthy testosterone levels.
